deviations.description: Pre-registered one-hop, no-path-reranker and random-path controls completed after the single locked test execution rather than before it.
deviations.impact: No test-driven retuning occurred and the locked output was not rerun, but the graph test is treated as descriptive rather than a positive confirmatory claim.
deviations.mitigation: Report all control results. Do not claim benefit because random-path and no-reranker controls outperform the full scorer on dev. A future study must redesign and freeze scoring before a new untouched test release.

deviations.description: The single locked fallback run used the frozen v1 retriever with at most two hops. Three-hop traversal was implemented and unit-tested only after the run.
deviations.impact: The reported locked metrics do not establish 4-node/three-hop capability; low 4-node F1 must be interpreted as a v1 limitation.
deviations.mitigation: Do not rerun or retune on the same locked split. Validate the three-hop implementation on dev and use a future untouched release for confirmation.

deviations.description: The v7 dev pilot was stopped before completion after audit found an undocumented 600-character per-abstract crop and incomplete X2 sham paths.
deviations.impact: The 78 evidence snapshots and 308 generated arm outputs are design-pilot artifacts only and cannot support the final graph-value claim.
deviations.mitigation: A frozen dev-300 component experiment selected full abstracts under the 1,800-word global budget. X2 now uses structurally valid unlinked background paths; ITT and complete-control sensitivity are both mandatory. E5-v8 must be rerun before any locked evaluation.

deviations.description: A strict post-budget audit of the completed v8 dev run found 10/43 graph-positive X2 questions with malformed visible paths; one also lost one of five sham-path slots, although every stored pre-budget control_complete flag was true.
deviations.impact: All arms retained equal total word budgets and generation had no failures, but X2 was not a structurally valid matched control. The entire v8 run is therefore a design pilot and cannot be the final E5 ablation.
deviations.mitigation: Graph paths are now atomic and never truncated; X1 and X2 have separate post-budget validators for target coverage, type, word length, hop count, canonical path syntax, and forbidden-node overlap. A clean v9 dev run is required before locked evaluation.
